'''Susevfi''', also known as '''Suarbi 7/5''', was a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Suarbi 7]].

Settled by [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]] expatriates in [[400 BBY/Legends|400 BBY]], its history is unknown until the arrival of [[Nikkos Tyris]], an [[Anzat (species)|Anzat]] [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] and minion of [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]]. Tyris established the [[Jensaarai/Legends|Jensaarai]] cult, with himself as first [[Saarai-kaar]], but his [[Death/Legends|death]] at the hands of [[Nejaa Halcyon]] would keep the Jensaarai hidden.

At some point, Susevfi came under the control of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Shortly before [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], Susevfi's Imperial [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] was killed by [[Leonia Tavira]] after he refused her sexual advances. Her [[Invids|Invid]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] made Susevfi their center of operations and coerced the Jensaarai into cooperation by threat of force.

[[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] eventually [[Battle of Susevfi|freed]] the Jensaarai from their service to the Invids. The planet was then brought into the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Some Jensaarai chose to join the [[New Jedi Order]]. Horn's friend [[Elegos A'Kla]] relocated the [[Caamasi Remnant]] (of which he was [[Trustant]]) from [[Kerilt]] to Susevfi and represented the moon directly in the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]]. When he was murdered by [[Shedao Shai]], he was replaced in the Senate by his daughter, [[Releqy A'Kla]], who later served on the [[High Council (Galactic Alliance)|High Council]] of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]]. Susevfi's main city was a seaport known as [[Yumfla]].
